subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI, Sherpa/OCCRP, HRW) + international press; Congolese state media EXCLUDED there. This is Congo-Brazzaville, NOT the DRC.

Denis Sassou Nguesso (in power since 1979 with a 1992-97 gap) was re-elected to a fifth term with ~95% in a March 2026 vote the opposition boycotted, conducted under a nationwide internet blackout and pre-poll arrests.

Standout drags: a 42-year personalist incumbency with an unresolved succession, acute debt distress (~97% GDP), family-level grand corruption (the 'Biens mal acquis' cases), and a documented 2024 torture/detention surge; the CFA euro peg is the lone offset.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored but debt-distressed, declining-oil rentier refinancing via costly Eurobonds.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~97.2% of GDP and in distress with fresh arrears and a 2025-26 payment peak refinanced via costly Eurobonds against falling oil. (IMF 2026; Reuters; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: The CEMAC CFA euro peg with a French convertibility guarantee - stable but non-sovereign. (BEAC; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is regional, not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored near the CEMAC norm. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: COBAC-supervised but heavily exposed to sovereign treasuries - regional-bank appetite for Congolese paper is a flagged IMF risk. (IMF 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Oil-dependent (the Pointe-Noire hub) with declining output, a gas-investment pivot, and minimal diversification - a resource curse. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: No judicial independence, family-level grand corruption, and a repressed press.

rule_of_law.notes: No judicial independence and pervasive grand corruption (CPI ~23/100), with active foreign ill-gotten-gains and Petronor-bribery cases against the ruling family. (TI 2024; Sherpa; Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak but IMF-engaged on macro data; political data unreliable. (IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Pervasive self-censorship and election-period repression (Pointe-Noire comms cut, journalists detained). (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: A 42-year incumbency re-entrenched by a boycotted vote, with a documented abuse surge and high poverty.

political_stability.notes: A 42-year incumbent re-entrenched at ~95% via a boycotted March 2026 vote with an internet blackout - suppressed instability with an acute succession overhang. (Al Jazeera; African Arguments)

civil_liberties.notes: A sharp documented rise in torture/arbitrary detention (CAD: 122 torture cases H1-2024 vs 13 prior). (Freedom House 2025; CAD/press)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not rights-protective, with a history of mass expulsions of DRC nationals. (HRW; OHCHR)

social_cohesion.notes: Poverty ~52% despite oil, with lingering (now dormant) Pool-region tensions. (World Bank; HRW)

demographics.notes: Young and highly urbanised - a potential dividend against weak jobs. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A China-indebted hedger with a dormant insurgency and Eurobond transfer risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: Pragmatic France/China hedging with heavy Chinese-debt entanglement; no bloc drift. (AidData; press)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war; the Pool insurgency dormant since the 2017 ceasefire. (HRW; ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not internationally sanctioned, within CEMAC FX, but Eurobond reliance amid thin reserves raises transfer risk. (IMF 2026; BEAC)

physical_practical.notes: Weak healthcare and infrastructure with flood-prone river cities.

climate.notes: Coastal flood/erosion on a low Atlantic shore, with oil-dependent low readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: A weak system (HDI ~153, medium-low) with thin services outside the two main cities. (World Bank; UNDP)

infrastructure.notes: Oil-funded pockets (the Pointe-Noire port, Brazzaville) against broadly poor power and roads. (World Bank)

summary: A textbook resource-cursed personalist state (Congo-Brazzaville, not the DRC): Denis Sassou Nguesso, in power since 1979, just secured a fifth term with ~95% in a March 2026 election the opposition boycotted, conducted under a nationwide internet blackout and pre-poll arrests. The economy is in outright debt distress (~97% of GDP, fresh arrears, a payment peak refinanced with risky Eurobonds against declining Pointe-Noire oil) and tarred by family-level grand corruption (the ill-gotten-gains and Petronor cases). Institutions offer no judicial independence and a repressed press, with a documented 2024 surge in torture and arbitrary detention. The CFA euro peg and low inflation are the only structural offsets; the skew is firmly negative, with an unresolved succession looming over an octogenarian incumbent.
